problem_solved:
The proof establishes a computable countable structure \(\mathcal A\) in a finite relational language with universe \(\omega\), whose automorphism group is exactly
\[
\{\alpha_F:F\subseteq\omega\text{ finite}\},
\]
where \(\alpha_F\) toggles the \(U\)-sort by symmetric difference with \(F\) and swaps the two \(V\)-rays over precisely the labels in \(F\).

It further establishes that for every copy \(\mathcal B\cong\mathcal A\) and every automorphism \(\rho\in\operatorname{Aut}(\mathcal B)\), one has
\[
\rho\leq_T D(\mathcal B),
\]
nonuniformly in \(\rho\). Hence \(\mathcal A\) is computably \(\operatorname{Aut}\)-countable on a cone, with cone base \(C=\emptyset\).

Finally, for every finite parameter tuple \(\bar a\), the proof chooses a label \(l\) whose \(V\)-gadget contains no element of \(\bar a\), sets \(\pi=\alpha_{\{l\}}\), and proves that \(\operatorname{Graph}(\pi)\) is not definable over
\[
\operatorname{rng}(\bar a)\cup \pi[\operatorname{rng}(\bar a)]
\]
by any countable disjunction of existential formulas of the stated \(\Sigma^{in}_1\) form. The key argument is the strong self-embedding shifting one ray of the \(l\)-gadget, which transfers every existential truth from the true graph pair \((r_0,r_1)\) to the false pair \((s_0,r_1)\).

The proof establishes a positive answer to the original question. It constructs a computable countable structure in a finite relational language with universe coded by \(\mathbb N\), with sorts \(L=\omega\), \(U=[\omega]^{<\omega}\), and \(V=\omega\times\{0,1\}\times\omega\). Its automorphism group is exactly \(\{\alpha_F:F\in[\omega]^{<\omega}\}\), where \(\alpha_F\) fixes labels, translates \(U\) by symmetric difference with \(F\), and swaps the two rays over precisely the labels in \(F\).

The proof also establishes that every automorphism of every copy \(\mathcal B\cong\mathcal A\) is Turing-computable from the complete atomic diagram of \(\mathcal B\), nonuniformly in the automorphism, by hardwiring its finite support and the relevant ray roots. Thus \(\mathcal A\) is computably \(\operatorname{Aut}\)-countable on a cone, with base \(0\).

Finally, for every finite parameter tuple \(\bar a\), the proof chooses a fresh label \(l\) whose \(V\)-gadget contains no parameter, takes \(\pi=\alpha_{\{l\}}\), and uses a strong self-embedding shifting one ray in \(P_l\) to show that no countable family of existential formulas over \(\bar a\cup\pi(\bar a)\) can define the graph of \(\pi\).

minor_gaps_text:
PATCHES REQUIRED FOR CORRECTNESS:
No patches required.
problem_solved: The proof establishes that there is a computable countable first-order structure, indeed in a finite relational language, whose automorphism group consists exactly of finite-support coordinate flips indexed by finite subsets of \(\omega\). For every copy \(\mathcal B\cong\mathcal A\), every automorphism of \(\mathcal B\) is Turing-computable from the complete atomic diagram of \(\mathcal B\) in the standard nonuniform sense, so the structure is computably \(\operatorname{AUT}\)-countable on a cone with computable/empty cone base. Moreover, for every finite parameter tuple \(\bar a\), choosing a fresh gadget \(P_n\) disjoint from \(\bar a\) and taking the automorphism \(\pi=\alpha_{\{n\}}\), the graph of \(\pi\) is not definable over \(\operatorname{rng}(\bar a)\cup\pi[\operatorname{rng}(\bar a)]\) by any countable family of existential formulas. Thus the original question receives a positive answer.
